source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure API Keys Are Restricted to Only APIs That Application Needs Access

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure API Keys Are Restricted to Only APIs That Application Needs Access
input_row.description: API Keys should only be used for services in cases where other authentication methods are unavailable. API keys are always at risk because they can be viewed publicly, such as from within a browser, or they can be accessed on a device where the key resides. It is recommended to restrict API keys to use (call) only APIs required by an application.
input_row.rationale: Security risks involved in using API-Keys are below: • API keys are simple encrypted strings • API keys do not identify the user or the application making the API request • API keys are typically accessible to clients, making it easy to discover and steal an API key In light of these potential risks, Google recommends using the standard authentication flow instead of API-Keys. However, there are limited cases where API keys are more appropriate. For example, if there is a mobile application that needs to use the Google Cloud Translation API, but doesn't otherwise need a backend server, API keys are the simplest way to authenticate to that API. In order to reduce attack surfaces by providing least privileges, API-Keys can be restricted to use (call) only APIs required by an application. Impact: Setting API restrictions may break existing application functioning, if not done carefully.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Go to APIs & Services\Credentials using https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials 2. In the section API Keys, Click the API Key Name. The API Key properties display on a new page. 3. For every API Key, ensure the section Key restrictions parameter API restrictions is not set to None. Or, Ensure API restrictions is not set to Google Cloud APIs Note: Google Cloud APIs represents the API collection of all cloud services/APIs offered by Google cloud. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all API Keys. gcloud services api-keys list Each key should have a line that says restrictions: followed by varying parameters and NOT have a line saying - service: cloudapis.googleapis.com as shown here restrictions: apiTargets: - service: cloudapis.googleapis.com
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Go to APIs & Services\Credentials using https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials 2. In the section API Keys, Click the API Key Name. The API Key properties display on a new page. 3. In the Key restrictions section go to API restrictions. 4. Click the Select API drop-down to choose an API. 5. Click Save. 6. Repeat steps 2,3,4,5 for every unrestricted API key Note: Do not set API restrictions to Google Cloud APIs, as this option allows access to all services offered by Google cloud. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all API keys. gcloud services api-keys list 2. Note the UID of the key to add restrictions to. 3. Run the update command with the appropriate API target service or flags file with API target services and methods to add the required restrictions. Command with appropriate API target service: gcloud services api-keys update <UID> --api-target=service=<service> Command with flags file: gcloud services api-keys update <UID> --flags-file=<flags_file>.yaml Content of flags file: - --api-target: service: "foo.service.com" - --api-target: service: "bar.service.com" methods: - "foomethod" - "barmethod" Note: Flags can be found by running: gcloud services api-keys update --help Note: Services can be found by running: gcloud services list or in this documentation https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/services/api-keys/update Default Value: By default, API restrictions are set to None. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/api-keys 2. https://cloud.google.com/apis/docs/overview
input_row.impact: Setting API restrictions may break existing application functioning, if not done carefully.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control is centered around ensuring API keys are restricted to only necessary APIs, which is a property that can be queried through GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud).
- The audit includes detailed steps using the Google Cloud Console and specific gcloud CLI commands to list API keys and check their restrictions, confirming that these checks can be automated.
- The referenced documentation provides the necessary guidance to perform the required checks programmatically.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_api_keys_restriction_enforced

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands to list and examine API keys and their restrictions:
  - `gcloud services api-keys list`: This command lists all the API keys available in the project, noting the details of restrictions applied.
- For each API key listed:
  - Check if the API restrictions are set to a specific API and not to the generic `cloudapis.googleapis.com`, which indicates no restrictions.
  - Check the `restrictions` field in the output to ensure it contains specific targeted API services for each key, i.e., it should not list `- service: cloudapis.googleapis.com`.
- Resources to enumerate include all API keys within the Google Cloud project being audited.
- Pass Criteria: Each API key should have one or more specific API targets listed under its restrictions and should not include `- service: cloudapis.googleapis.com`.
- Example check: Utilize the command `gcloud services api-keys list` and parse through the output to ensure there is no unrestricted key indicated by the absence of specific `apiTargets`.
- Regularly validate API key restrictions to ensure compliance with security policies and reduce potential exposure.
step2_review:
---

### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. The restriction status of API keys can be programmatically queried using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud). There is no need for manual review unless the automation output is ambiguous.
- **GCP SDK Capabilities:**  
  The `gcloud services api-keys list` command, as well as the underlying [API Keys API](https://cloud.google.com/api-keys/docs/reference/rest), provide access to all necessary data for this control.
- **GCP CLI Commands:**  
  The gcloud CLI supports both listing and describing API keys, and the restrictions can be parsed from the output.
- **Other Automation Options:**  
  While GCP Config (now called "Config Controller") does not natively support API key restriction checks, automation via gcloud or direct API calls is fully supported.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 8/10**

- **Automation Details:**  
  The use of `gcloud services api-keys list` is correct for enumerating API keys. However, this command only provides a summary. To check detailed restrictions, you must use `gcloud services api-keys describe <KEY_ID>` for each key, as the `list` output may not include full restriction details.
- **Pass Criteria:**  
  The logic for checking that no key is unrestricted (i.e., no specific `apiTargets` or only `cloudapis.googleapis.com`) is sound.
- **Program Name:**  
  The program name `gcp_api_keys_restriction_enforced` is descriptive but could be more explicit (see Improvements).
- **Completeness:**  
  The assessment misses a critical detail: API keys can also have **application restrictions** (HTTP referrers, IPs, Android/iOS apps) in addition to API restrictions. Best practice is to check for both.
- **Accuracy:**  
  The check for `cloudapis.googleapis.com` is correct, but the logic should also ensure that the `apiTargets` field is not empty or missing, which would indicate no API restriction at all.

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Command Usage:**  
  - **List**: Use `gcloud services api-keys list` to enumerate keys.
  - **Describe**: For each key, run `gcloud services api-keys describe <KEY_ID>` to get full restriction details (including both API and application restrictions).
- **Check Both Restriction Types:**  
  - Ensure **API restrictions** are set (i.e., `apiTargets` is not empty and does not include only `cloudapis.googleapis.com`).
  - Ensure **application restrictions** are set (e.g., HTTP referrers, IPs, Android/iOS apps), as per CIS recommendations.
- **Program Name Suggestion:**  
  - More explicit: `gcp_api_keys_api_restriction_enforced`
  - Even better if including both types: `gcp_api_keys_api_and_app_restriction_enforced`
- **Alternative API Methods:**  
  - Use the [API Keys API](https://cloud.google.com/api-keys/docs/reference/rest/v2/projects.locations.keys/get) directly for automation at scale.
- **Missing Considerations:**  
  - Alert if any key has no API restrictions (`apiTargets` missing or empty).
  - Alert if any key has no application restrictions.
  - Document and handle service accounts that may have API keys (rare, but possible).
- **Output Parsing:**  
  - Recommend outputting results in JSON for easier parsing and automation.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The assessment is fundamentally correct and aligns with GCP capabilities. Minor improvements are needed for completeness and best practice alignment.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **MODIFY**

- **What to Modify:**
  - Update the automation steps to include both `list` and `describe` commands for each key.
  - Explicitly check for both API and application restrictions.
  - Update the program name for clarity, e.g., `gcp_api_keys_api_and_app_restriction_enforced`.
  - Clarify that the absence of any restriction (API or application) is non-compliant.
  - Recommend output in JSON for robust automation.

---

**Summary Table:**

| Section               | Assessment        | Notes                                                                                  |
|-----------------------|------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation   | AGREE            | Automated is correct; GCP SDK/CLI supports this.                                       |
| Technical Accuracy    | 8/10             | Correct, but misses need for `describe` and application restrictions.                  |
| Improvements          | See above        | Add `describe`, check both restriction types, improve program name, output in JSON.    |
| Confidence            | HIGH             | GCP supports this; minor improvements needed.                                          |
| Final Recommendation  | MODIFY           | Update automation details and program name as described.                               |

---
